extracted_rules.condition: If athlete trains for any endurance event and no specific methodology is prescribed
extracted_rules.action: Distribute at least 80% of weekly training TIME at Zone 1-2 (below first ventilatory threshold) and no more than 20% at Zone 3+
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Z1-2: ≥80% of weekly training time; Z3+: ≤20%; measured in minutes, not distance or sessions
extracted_rules.source: Fitzgerald 80/20 Running + Seiler 2010 polarized model meta-analysis

extracted_rules.rule_name: Polarized TID — VO2max and work economy
extracted_rules.condition: If objective is to improve VO2max and work economy in endurance runners
extracted_rules.action: Apply polarized intensity distribution: 15-20% high-intensity, 75-80% low-intensity
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: HIT: 15-20% of weekly volume; LIT: 75-80%; threshold work: remaining 5-10%
extracted_rules.source: Systematic review: polarized TID vs threshold TID in endurance athletes

extracted_rules.rule_name: VDOT — derive training paces from race performance
extracted_rules.condition: If athlete has a recent race time (within 4-6 weeks) for any standard distance
extracted_rules.action: Look up VDOT from Daniels Table 5.1 using race distance + time, then derive all training paces (E/M/T/I/R) from Table 5.2
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: VDOT = lookup(race_distance, race_time) → E_pace, M_pace, T_pace, I_pace, R_pace from Table 5.2
extracted_rules.source: Daniels' Running Formula, 3rd ed., Tables 5.1 and 5.2

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete has a recent mile time but no full race time for VDOT lookup, and VDOT > 50
extracted_rules.action: Use mile race pace as R-pace; each slower zone adds 6 sec/400m: R → I (+6s) → T (+12s)
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: I_pace = R_pace + 6s per 400m; T_pace = I_pace + 6s per 400m; for VDOT 40-50 use 7-8s; R is fastest

extracted_rules.condition: If scheduling an easy or recovery run
extracted_rules.action: Run at fully conversational effort; no HR cap specified; this forms 60-75% of weekly mileage
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Conversational pace; ~59-74% vVO2max; 60-75% of total weekly volume at this intensity

extracted_rules.rule_name: Threshold pace (T-pace) zone and volume cap
extracted_rules.condition: If scheduling a tempo or lactate threshold session
extracted_rules.action: Run at 'comfortably hard' controlled effort; cap T-pace volume at 10% of weekly mileage per session
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: ~83-88% vVO2max; max T-volume per session = weekly_mileage × 10%

extracted_rules.rule_name: Interval pace (I-pace) zone and volume cap
extracted_rules.condition: If scheduling interval work targeting VO2max
extracted_rules.action: Run 3-5 min work bouts at I-pace; recovery ≥ work duration; cap session volume
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: ~95-100% vVO2max; work bouts: 3-5 min; recovery: ≥ work duration; max I-volume = min(10 km, weekly_mileage × 8%)

extracted_rules.rule_name: Repetition pace (R-pace) zone and volume cap
extracted_rules.condition: If scheduling speed and running economy work (200-400m reps)
extracted_rules.action: Run 200-400m at R-pace; near-full recovery (2-3× work duration); cap session volume
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: ~105-120% vVO2max; work bouts: ≤2 min; recovery: 2-3× work duration; max R-volume = min(8 km, weekly_mileage × 5%)

extracted_rules.condition: Always — when increasing weekly mileage
extracted_rules.action: Never increase weekly mileage by more than 10% over the previous week; hold new level 3-4 weeks before next increase
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: new_weekly_mileage ≤ previous_week_mileage × 1.10; hold 3-4 weeks before next increase
extracted_rules.source: Daniels' Running Formula §2; Pfitzinger Advanced Marathoning Ch.2; consensus across all 5 books

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete is on a marathon training plan
extracted_rules.action: Cap long run distance and duration using the most conservative binding rule
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: long_run ≤ min(weekly_mileage × 0.29, 22 miles) AND projected_duration ≤ 210 min (3.5 h); Pfitz-Adv rule is primary
extracted_rules.source: Pfitzinger Advanced Marathoning; Daniels (25-30% + 150 min cap); INDEX.md conflict resolution

extracted_rules.rule_name: Long run ceiling — 5K to half-marathon plan
extracted_rules.condition: If athlete is on a 5K, 10K, or half-marathon training plan
extracted_rules.action: Cap long run at 20-25% of weekly mileage; no absolute distance cap for these distances
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: long_run ≤ weekly_mileage × 0.20-0.25; no absolute mi cap for sub-marathon
extracted_rules.source: Pfitzinger Faster Road Racing; INDEX.md coverage matrix

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete has trained at consistent volume for 3-4 weeks
extracted_rules.action: Schedule a cutback week with 20-25% volume reduction; maintain intensity, reduce mileage only
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Every 3-4 weeks: volume reduction = 20-25%; intensity sessions unchanged
extracted_rules.source: Pfitzinger Advanced Marathoning Ch.2; Pfitzinger Faster Road Racing

extracted_rules.condition: Always — when scheduling weekly training
extracted_rules.action: Limit quality sessions (containing T/I/R pace work or Z4-5) to ≤3/week for all athletes; Z4-5 sessions hard-capped at 2/week regardless of level
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Q-days ≤ 3/week; Z4-5 sessions ≤ 2/week (all athletes); never schedule 2 consecutive hard days
extracted_rules.source: Daniels' Running Formula §2; Fitzgerald 80/20 Ch.3; INDEX.md conflict resolution

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete returns after a break of ≤5 days
extracted_rules.action: Resume at 100% of previous training load; no adjustment needed
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Break ≤5 days → resume at 100% load; no VDOT adjustment

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete returns after a break of 6-28 days
extracted_rules.action: Resume at 50% load for first half of return period, then 75% for second half; adjust VDOT by -1 to -3 points
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Break 6-28 days → first half at 50%, second half at 75%; VDOT adjustment: ×0.93-0.99

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete returns after a break of more than 8 weeks
extracted_rules.action: Start at 33-50% of previous load; recalculate VDOT from a recent time trial; expect significant fitness decay
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Break >8 wk → start at 33-50% load; VDOT × 0.80-0.92; new race or time trial required to reset paces

extracted_rules.condition: If training at altitude ≥7,000 feet (2,133 m)
extracted_rules.action: Keep R-pace unchanged; adjust I and T sessions by effort (not pace); expect paces to be 3-5% slower at same physiological cost
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Altitude ≥7,000 ft: R_pace unchanged; I and T → run by effort; expected pace slowing: 3-5%

extracted_rules.condition: Before any T, I, or R-pace session
extracted_rules.action: Run 10-15 min at E-pace, then 4-6 strides (20-30 sec at R-pace effort); cool down 10-15 min E-pace after session
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Warm-up: 10-15 min E-pace + 4-6 strides × 20-30s at R-effort; cool-down: 10-15 min E-pace
extracted_rules.source: Daniels' Running Formula §2; Pfitzinger Advanced Marathoning Ch.3

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete is tapering for a marathon (A-race)
extracted_rules.action: Apply 3-week taper: each week reduce volume by 20%, 40%, 60% from peak; maintain intensity throughout
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Week -3: volume × 0.80; Week -2: volume × 0.60; Week -1 (race week): volume × 0.40; intensity sessions maintained

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete substitutes cycling or pool running for road running sessions
extracted_rules.action: Apply equivalency factor to compare aerobic load; maintain 80/20 TID across all modalities
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Cycling: 1.5× bike miles = 1 run mile (aerobic equivalency); pool running: target HR −10% vs equivalent road run HR

extracted_rules.condition: If scheduling a marathon-pace run or goal marathon-pace segment within a long run
extracted_rules.action: Run at current goal marathon pace; embed M-pace in long runs or as standalone medium-long runs; effort is controlled and sustainable for 26.2 miles
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: M-pace: 75-84% vVO2max; 75-84% HRmax; volume embedded in long run = unlimited; standalone M-pace run capped at 29% of weekly mileage

extracted_rules.condition: When structuring a seasonal training plan for a runner
extracted_rules.action: Apply Daniels 4-phase structure: Phase I (Foundation/E+L), Phase II (Repetition/R), Phase III (Interval/I), Phase IV (Threshold/T); compress proportionally if season <24 weeks
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Phase I: E-pace + Long runs; Phase II: R-pace reps (economy); Phase III: I-pace intervals (VO2max); Phase IV: T-pace tempo (threshold); ideal duration: 6 weeks/phase; season 16-24 weeks: compress each phase to 4 weeks; season <16 weeks: use Phases III-IV only (skip I-II)
extracted_rules.source: Daniels' Running Formula, 3rd ed., §5 Training Plans

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete is following a FIRST (Run Less, Run Faster) training model
extracted_rules.action: Prescribe exactly 3 key runs per week: Track Repeats (TR, Z4-5), Tempo Run (TMP, Z3), Long Run (LR, Z1-2); mandate 2 non-weight-bearing cross-training sessions per week; adding a 4th run defeats the model
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: TR: 5K pace or faster (Z4-5, 1 session); TMP: 10K-HM pace (Z3, 1 session); LR: goal_marathon_pace + 45-90s/mile but not slower than E-pace for athlete's VDOT (Z1-2, 1 session); XT: 2x/week non-weight-bearing (cycling, swimming); 4th run = model violation
extracted_rules.source: Pierce, Murr & Moss, Run Less Run Faster (FIRST program)

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete is on a 5K or 10K plan and scheduling a VO2max-targeted interval session
extracted_rules.action: Prescribe 600m-1 mile repeats at 5K race pace with full recovery intervals; use only during 5K/10K-specific plan weeks, not marathon prep
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Rep distance: 600m-1 mile at 5K race pace; recovery: full jog equal duration; reps: 4-6; total VO2max volume: 4-6 km per session; not applicable in marathon or >10K blocks

extracted_rules.condition: When scheduling a medium-effort aerobic run that is not a recovery run and not a quality session
extracted_rules.action: Assign General Aerobic pace: MP+15-25% slower than goal marathon pace; fills the majority of non-quality weekly mileage; never slower than E-pace
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: GA_pace = marathon_pace_min_per_mile x 1.15 to x 1.25; example: 8:00/mile marathon pace -> GA pace 9:12-10:00/mile; HR: approximately 73-84% HRmax; typical run duration: 60-110 min

extracted_rules.condition: If a quality session (T/I/R-pace) is scheduled and weather is adverse (high wind, ice, extreme heat)
extracted_rules.action: Swap the quality day with a scheduled E-day; perform the quality session when weather improves; acceptable substitutes: fartlek or hard hill repeats to achieve similar physiological stimulus
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Option A: swap Q-day with E-day in same week; Option B: substitute fartlek (unstructured) or hill repeats (same CV stimulus without pace precision required); pace targets secondary to physiological effort
extracted_rules.source: Daniels' Running Formula, 3rd ed., §3-4 (workout flexibility and environmental adaptation guidance)

extracted_rules.condition: If athlete is tapering for a 5K, 10K, or half-marathon
extracted_rules.action: Apply distance-specific taper: shorter taper for 5K, longer for half-marathon; reduce volume progressively while maintaining intensity
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: 5K taper: 1-2 weeks; Week-1: -20-25%, Week-2 (race week): -35-40%; 10K taper: 2 weeks; Week-1: -20-25%, Week-2: -35-40%; HM taper: 2-3 weeks; Week-1: -20-25%, Week-2: -35-40%, Week-3: -50-60%; intensity sessions maintained throughout

extracted_rules.rule_name: Return from break — Pfitz protocol (>20 days)
extracted_rules.condition: If athlete returns after a break of more than 20 days (Pfitz-Adv model override: takes precedence over default medium-break protocol for breaks 20-28 days)
extracted_rules.action: Skip all missed sessions entirely; revise goal race time downward; do not attempt to make up lost volume; resume with easy weeks and reassess fitness before setting new goals
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Break >20 days (Pfitz-Adv): zero makeup sessions (overrides default 50%/75% medium-break protocol); weeks 1-2: Z1-Z2 only at 50-60% of pre-break volume; revise goal race time; reassess pace targets after 3-4 weeks of resumed training

extracted_rules.condition: If an athlete following the 80/20 model returns after a break of more than 2 weeks
extracted_rules.action: First week back at 50% of pre-break volume; maintain 80/20 TID from the first session back; reduce volume, not intensity distribution
extracted_rules.formula_or_value: Break >2 weeks: week 1 = 50% volume, full 80/20 TID; week 2 = 70% volume; week 3 = 85%; week 4 = full volume if no adverse HRV or fatigue signals
